flags.concern: timeline-canvas-tests: Step 2 changes `TimelineCanvas.tsx` to read `pendingOpsRef` from `useTimelineEditorData()`, but the plan never updates the existing test harness that mocks only `useTimelineEditorContext`. If that mock is left as-is, the resize pending-ops tests will fail as soon as the component import changes.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` imports and uses `useTimelineEditorContext` today at lines 21 and 275, while `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` defines `useTimelineEditorContextMock` at line 8, wires only `useTimelineEditorContext` in the module mock at lines 10-18, and injects `pendingOpsRef` through that mock at line 43.

flags.concern: timeline-state-types: Step 5 treats `TimelineApplyEdit` as something callers can import directly from `useTimelineCommit`, but that module does not export a `TimelineApplyEdit` alias today. The alias is defined only in `useTimelineData.types.ts`, so following the mapping literally leaves several hooks without a source type unless the refactor first creates a new shared export for it.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts` exports `TimelineEditMutation` and `ApplyEditOptions` at lines 46-57 and exposes the function signature inline on `UseTimelineCommitResult.applyEdit` at line 71, but `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.types.ts` defines `TimelineApplyEdit` itself at lines 36-39. Current consumers include `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts` lines 18-21, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts` lines 11-17, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts` lines 17-23, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` line 9.

flags.concern: timeline-state-types: The plan's default destination for the moved shared types, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, is the wrong layer in this repository. That file already depends on `useAssetManagement`, `useClipEditing`, `useClipResize`, `useDragCoordinator`, `useExternalDrop`, and `useTimelineTrackManagement` to build top-level context value types, so moving low-level shared aliases there would make those hooks depend back on the state-assembly module and introduce a circular type graph. A dedicated leaf types module is the safer destination.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts` imports the hook modules at lines 1-7 and derives `ReturnType<typeof ...>` aliases at lines 37-43. Those hook files currently consume the shared aliases from `useTimelineData.types.ts`, for example `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts` lines 18-21 and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts` lines 11-17.

flags.concern: timeline-typecheck: The verification strategy is too weak for Step 6. Deleting `useTimelineData.types.ts` is mostly a TypeScript import-rewiring change, but the plan only runs the focused video-editor vitest slice and grep checks. This repo's own build path depends on `tsc`, so the plan needs an explicit compile check after the type move; otherwise type-only consumers can break without being imported by the targeted tests.
flags.evidence: `reigh-app/package.json` runs `tsc` in `build` at line 11 and defines a dedicated typecheck script at lines 40-42. The deletion target is referenced through type-only imports such as `src/tools/video-editor/lib/bulk-utils.ts` line 1 and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` line 9, which are not guaranteed by the stated test command alone.
